medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Patient initially presented to the emergency department for evaluation of abdominal discomfort, generalized weakness, nausea and vomiting over the past 5 days. Reports blood pressure of 60/40 and tachycardic to about 120. Reports losing weight about 65 pounds over several years, with vomiting for around 10 months which has been increasing over the last few days. Denies fever, chills. Reports occasional night sweats. At the hospital, patient was found to have a pulmonary mass, pleural effusion, rib lesion, and liver tumors.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Hypertension, diabetes, intellectual disability, type 2 diabetes, breast cancer currently in remission, DVTs on Coumadin, major depressive disorder in full remission, eczema, osteoarthritis of hip, hypokalemia.

physical_examination.general_examination: Vital signs: Temp 97.5 °F, BP 137/86, HR 95, RR 18, O2 sat 95% RA. Patient is cachectic. Abdominal examination is nondistended, nontender in all quadrants. No rebound or guarding.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neurological: A&Ox3, CN intact, strength and sensation intact throughout. Cardiovascular: RRR, nl S1/S2, no murmurs, gallops, or rubs. Lungs: CTAB, decreased BS.
